original_title: A Study of Disitamab Vedotin in Previously Treated Solid Tumors That Express HER2
title: Disitamab vedotin's HER2 tumor basket, still running
blurb: A phase 2 trial of disitamab vedotin, Seagen/Pfizer's HER2-targeted ADC, across HER2-expressing solid tumors including non-small-cell lung and ovarian cancer. Active but no longer recruiting, so this is a status marker more than a data drop.

original_title: Antibody-Drug Conjugates Against Multidrug-Resistant Cancers: Biomarker-Guided Patient Selection, Payload Engineering, Linker Chemistry, and Bystander Effects.
title: The ADC toolbox against drug-resistant cancers
blurb: A review of the levers for making ADCs work against multidrug-resistant tumors: biomarker-guided patient selection, payload engineering, linker chemistry, and bystander killing. Survey piece, not new data.

original_title: Deciphering the phenotypic responses to (177)Lu-based radioligand therapy to identify efficacious combinations.
title: Reading tumor cells' response to Lu-177 for clues on what to pair it with
blurb: Profiles how tumor cells respond phenotypically to Lu-177-based radioligand therapy, then uses that to flag which combination partners are likely to help. Rational combo-picking rather than another head-to-head trial.
venue: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging

original_title: Beyond PSMA with STEAP1, DLL3, and H7B3 as Emerging Radioligand Targets - Renu Eapen - UroToday
title: The targets lining up after PSMA
blurb: A rundown on STEAP1, DLL3, and H7B3 as the next wave of radioligand targets beyond PSMA. Commentary rather than new trial data, but a decent map of where the pipeline's heading.

original_title: Alkyne Migratory Insertion Enabled Dynamic Kinetic Asymmetric Dearomatization of Unactivated Racemic Biaryls
title: Turning racemic biaryls chiral through a Pd/alkyne insertion trick
blurb: A palladium-catalyzed dynamic kinetic asymmetric dearomatization that converts racemic, unactivated naphthalene-based biaryls into enantiopure 3D scaffolds via alkyne migratory insertion. Most DYKAT chemistry needs an activated substrate to start from; pulling it off on unactivated racemic biaryls is the actual news here, and it leans on a custom polyfluorinated phosphoramidite ligand to do it.

original_title: Automated radiosynthesis and biodistribution of [18F]fluoroflutamide
title: An 18F androgen-receptor tracer built to dodge the molar activity problem
blurb: Automated radiosynthesis and biodistribution of [18F]fluoroflutamide, meant to image androgen receptor expression by PET alongside Ga-68 PSMA scans, after earlier attempts at labeling enzalutamide stalled on low molar activity. Whether it clears that bar in humans isn't in yet.

original_title: Tribochemical Surface Modification of Silicon
title: Scratch silicon and it starts bonding on its own
blurb: Mechanical scratching activates silicon surfaces enough that thiol, alcohol, or alkyne-terminated molecules react with them spontaneously, in dilute solution, room temperature, no light, heat, or applied voltage. XPS and electrochemistry confirm the molecules actually attach. Friction doing the whole job of a reagent and a light source is the neat part here.

original_title: Total Synthesis of the Diterpene Alkaloid Thelepogine through Enyne Hydroamination
title: Thelepogine made via enyne hydroamination
blurb: A total synthesis of the diterpene alkaloid thelepogine built around a key enyne hydroamination step to assemble the core.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Identifying lipid-protein interactomes with functional lipid probes.
title: A protocol for mapping which proteins grab which lipids
blurb: A Nature Protocols method for building lipid-protein interactomes using functional lipid probes. This is a step-by-step you'd cite for the workflow, not a single finding.

original_title: Using enantioselective biosensors to evolve asymmetric biocatalysts
title: A transcription factor that lights up for the right enantiomer
blurb: d'Oelsnitz et al. engineer transcription factors that sense chiral reaction products and convert that into a fluorescent signal, then use it to evolve asymmetric enzymes toward inverted selectivity. Turns "which enantiomer did you make" into something you can sort cells on instead of running a chiral column every time.

original_title: Characterization and calibration of the iQID digital autoradiography system for direct quantitative imaging of beta-emitters in tissue samples.
title: Calibrating the iQID camera for counting beta decays in tissue
blurb: Characterization and calibration work for the iQID digital autoradiography system, for direct quantitative imaging of beta-emitters in tissue sections. A dosimetry methods paper, unglamorous but the kind people actually cite when their numbers need to hold up.

original_title: Chemical Synthesis of Asymmetric Nucleosomes Reveals Intramolecular Activation of UHRF1 E3 Ligase by H3K9me3
title: How H3K9me3 switches on UHRF1 from the inside
blurb: Chemically synthesized asymmetric nucleosomes, with the methyl mark on only one histone tail, show H3K9me3 activates UHRF1's E3 ligase intramolecularly rather than through a separate reader handing off to a writer. The kind of mechanistic question you can only ask once you can actually build a lopsided nucleosome.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Self-Splicing RNA Platform Encodes Twin PROTACs for Dual-E3 Ligase Recruitment and Programmable Protein Degradation
title: An RNA that splices itself into two PROTACs at once
blurb: A self-splicing RNA platform encodes twin PROTACs, each recruiting a different E3 ligase, off a single construct for programmable dual-degrader chemistry.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Theoretical Framework for Predicting Dynamic Covalent Exchange Based on Proton Affinities and Proton‐Transfer Assistance
title: Why some dynamic covalent bonds swap faster than others
blurb: DFT plus kinetics on acylsemicarbazide dynamic networks trace bond-exchange rates to proton affinities and how much a nearby proton transfer assists the dissociation step. Reads as a design-rule paper for tuning reprocessable dynamic networks, not a new material itself.

original_title: Novartis to invest KRW 140 B in Korea’s radioligand therapy ecosystem - BioSpectrum Asia
title: Novartis puts KRW 140B into Korea's radioligand therapy buildout
blurb: Novartis is investing roughly 140 billion won into radioligand therapy infrastructure in Korea. It's the company's own figure and a bet on future RLT demand, not a finished plant yet.

original_title: Mapping Proton‐Coupled Electron Transfer With Real Space Coordinates
title: Trying to give proton-coupled electron transfer an actual geometry
blurb: The standard square scheme for PCET plots electron transfer, proton transfer, and the coupled step by energy alone, with no structural information. This work tries to map PCET onto real-space coordinates instead, a conceptual reframing more than a new result, worth judging by whether people start using the coordinates.

original_title: Understanding Selectivities in Gold(I)-Catalyzed Intramolecular C(sp 3 )–H Bond Functionalization through Predictive Statistical Analysis
title: Predicting which C–H bond the gold catalyst picks
blurb: Gold(I) catalysts do intramolecular C(sp3)–H functionalization, and instead of the usual after-the-fact rationalization this builds a statistical model to predict which bond reacts and why. Only worth much if it forecasts new substrates rather than just explaining the ones already run.

original_title: Matthew Kurian: A New View on Radioligand Therapy for Breast Cancer from Novartis - Oncodaily
title: Novartis wants radioligand therapy in breast cancer next
blurb: A profile of Novartis's Matthew Kurian making the case for pushing radioligand therapy into breast cancer, a much bigger and messier indication than the prostate and neuroendocrine cancers the modality has actually proven itself in. Read it as a positioning piece, not a data drop.

original_title: Thermal Bimolecular Homodimerization Initiates Nickel(I) Catalysis from 2,6-Aryl-Unsubstituted Bipyridyl Nickel(II) Oxidative Addition Complexes
title: How bipyridyl-nickel catalysts bootstrap their own Ni(I)
blurb: Strip the bulky 2,6-aryl groups off the bipyridyl ligand and two Ni(II) oxidative-addition complexes just dimerize and comproportionate on their own, thermally generating the Ni(I) that actually does the catalysis. So the ligand sterics that everyone tunes for selectivity were also quietly gatekeeping this initiation pathway.

original_title: Selective Oxidation From Toluene to Benzaldehyde via 4 f‐ Orbital Involvement
title: Samarium doping stops toluene oxidation at the aldehyde
blurb: Sm-doped Bi2WO6 nanosheets push toluene's benzylic C–H to benzaldehyde at 8550 µmol·g⁻¹·h⁻¹ with over 86% selectivity, rather than running on to the acid. The doping generates oxygen vacancies and Bi–O frustrated Lewis pairs, and the authors credit the samarium's 4f orbitals for the dehydrogenation step. Good numbers for a nanosheet catalyst; nothing here yet on how it holds up over repeated cycles.

original_title: Reinventing the Mitsunobu Reaction with Iminophosphorane Reagents
title: A Mitsunobu reaction that skips the explosive azo reagent
blurb: Iminophosphoranes replace the classic DIAD/PPh3 pairing in the Mitsunobu reaction, made in situ from a cheap commercial azide, dodging the explosion risk and the ugly purifications everyone puts up with. They also claim it gets around the narrow pronucleophile pKa window that's kept Mitsunobu locked out of C–C bond formation, which if it holds is the actual news here, not just a safer reagent swap.

original_title: Host–Guest Mismatch Enables Selective Hydrogen Atom Transfer
title: A catalyst too big to fit inside its own host, and that's the trick
blurb: Cyclodextrins normally bind guests inside the cavity, but the polyoxometalate catalyst here is size-mismatched and sticks to the outside instead, and that exterior binding is what gives the decatungstate its site-selectivity in hydrogen atom transfer. A binding mode nobody much bothers with, put to work on purpose.

original_title: Design, Synthesis, Molecular Docking, and Preclinical Evaluation of a New Radiolabeled PEG3-Linked FAPI Derivative for Fibroblast Activation Protein Targeting.
title: A FAPI tracer with a PEG3 spacer bolted on
blurb: New radiolabeled FAPI derivative for fibroblast activation protein targeting, taken through docking and preclinical testing. Mice and modeling so far, no human data.

original_title: Resolving Conformational Chirality in a Stereolabile Macrocycle
title: Catching chirality in a macrocycle that isn't supposed to hold still
blurb: Conformational chirality in macrocycles is usually stereolabile, the forms interconverting too fast to pin down, and this one gets resolved anyway. Worth it for whatever trick let them trap the conformer long enough to characterize it.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Reactivity of Water Dimer Radical Cations toward Inert Molecules: DFT Approaches for the Realistic Calculation of Ground- and Excited-State Reaction Profiles
title: DFT wasn't built for radical cations, so this patches it
blurb: Standard quantum chemistry methods are parameterized on neutral closed-shell ground states, a poor fit for radical cations, so this works out better DFT approaches for modeling water-dimer radical cations reacting with otherwise-inert molecules, covering both ground- and excited-state profiles. A methods correction rather than a splashy result, but the kind a lot of downstream radical-cation papers are quietly depending on being right.

original_title: FMRP-LEAN: A HIPAA-Compliant AI-Augmented LIMS Architecture for End-to-End Clinical Assay Workflow Optimization
title: Getting Fragile X biomarker labs off spreadsheets
blurb: A HIPAA-compliant, AI-augmented LIMS architecture meant to replace the spreadsheet-and-manual-QC setup that multi-day assays like Luminex FMRP quantification usually run on. The pitch is less reporting delay and better state visibility between lab and clinical teams. Infrastructure, not a new assay.

original_title: Heat‐Induced Granular Motion in Quasi‐Solid‐State Suzuki–Miyaura Reaction
title: A Suzuki coupling that shakes itself awake with heat, no ball mill required
blurb: Solid-state cross-coupling usually needs grinding or milling to get insoluble reactants talking to each other. This one skips that: a polymeric palladium catalyst plus heat alone sets off granular motion that does the mixing, additive-free and with no mechanical input at all. Neat trick if it generalizes past whatever insoluble substrates they tried it on.

original_title: Yield Smarter, Not Harder: Good Practices for Machine Learning of Reaction Outcomes
title: A field guide to not fooling yourself with reaction-yield ML
blurb: A good-practices piece for machine learning applied to predicting reaction outcomes, the kind of paper meant to catch the usual traps rather than report a new model. Worth it if your yield-prediction benchmark has ever looked too good to be true.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Interfacial Ligand Engineering Breaks the Activity-Selectivity Trade-Off in Electrochemical Alkynol Semihydrogenation and -Deuteration
title: Breaking hydrogenation's activity vs selectivity trade-off with ligands at the interface
blurb: Electrochemical semihydrogenation of alkynols usually forces a choice between fast and selective. Interfacial ligand engineering reportedly gets both at once, and the same platform runs deuteration too, handy if you want site-specific deuterated alkenes instead of an isotope-scrambled mess.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Enzyme-Inspired Design of Well-Defined Dual-Metal-Site Pairs Confined within Metal–Organic Frameworks for Tailoring Photocatalytic Microenvironments
title: Giving MOFs matched metal pairs so photocatalysis behaves like an enzyme active site
blurb: Enzymes get their precision from metal pairs held at exact spacing; this builds well-defined dual-metal sites into MOFs to copy that trick and shape the photocatalytic microenvironment around them. Reads as a design framework more than a single flashy reaction.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Anaerobic time-resolved serial crystallography captures CO dissociation and rebinding in oxygen-sensitive -hydrogenase
title: Watching CO let go and come back in an enzyme that hates oxygen
blurb: Time-resolved serial crystallography needs a lot of handling, which is normally incompatible with the strict anaerobic conditions oxygen-sensitive hydrogenases demand. Here an anaerobic, room-temperature version of the method catches CO dissociating from and rebinding to the active site mid-catalysis, an actual movie of that intermediate instead of one inferred from spectroscopy.

original_title: An isolable nitrene radical anion as a nucleophilic open-shell nitrogen synthon
title: A nitrene radical anion stable enough to bottle, and it acts as a nucleophile
blurb: Nitrenes are normally too reactive to isolate, let alone hand off electrons the wrong way. This one is stable enough to isolate as a radical anion and still behaves as a nucleophilic nitrogen source, an odd pairing of properties for something this reactive on paper.

original_title: Datopotamab deruxtecan in solid tumors: Recent research progress and clinical applications.
title: Datopotamab deruxtecan's clinical scorecard so far
blurb: A review rounding up datopotamab deruxtecan's progress and clinical applications across solid tumors. It's a status check on where the trials stand, not new data.

original_title: Direct photoexcitation of borate complexes for radical generation: A platform from organic synthesis to chemical biology
title: Skipping the photocatalyst, borates that generate radicals on their own
blurb: Photoredox catalysis usually needs a catalyst shuttling electrons or energy through several steps just to make a radical. These borate complexes get excited directly by light and produce carbon-centered radicals without any of that machinery, and the authors carry the trick from plain organic synthesis into chemical biology.
venue: Proceedings of the Japan Academy Series B

original_title: Deciphering the phenotypic responses to 177Lu-based radioligand therapy to identify efficacious combinations
title: Mapping how tumors respond to Lu-177 radioligand therapy to find its best partner drug
blurb: A profiling study of the phenotypic responses tumors mount against 177Lu-based radioligand therapy, aimed at pointing toward which combination treatments would actually help rather than pairing drugs by guesswork.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: Synthesis of 3-Chalcogenylated Benzo[ b ]furans Enabled by a Multicomponent Reagent System
title: Building 3-chalcogenylated benzofurans in one pot with a multicomponent reagent
blurb: A multicomponent reagent system installs a chalcogen at the 3-position of benzo[b]furans in a single operation instead of a stepwise route. No scope data given here, so hard to say yet how far it stretches.

original_title: AI-redesigned starting points and outcomes enhance protein evolution
title: Design the starting protein before you evolve it
blurb: Directed evolution usually grinds through mutation rounds starting from a natural enzyme. This workflow starts from an AI-redesigned scaffold instead, and the enzymes that come out of it beat ones evolved from the natural version. Front-loading the design work so evolution has less ground to cover.

original_title: Countercation Manipulation Enables Selective Electrochemical CO 2 ‐to‐Formate Conversion Over Bi‐Polyoxometalate‐Derived Catalysts
title: The countercation is doing the selectivity work in CO2-to-formate
blurb: Bi-polyoxometalate catalysts for electrochemically reducing CO2 to formate, where the lever for pushing selectivity toward HCOO- turns out to be which countercation sits on the POM rather than the metal center itself. A cheap knob to tune, structure and composition left alone.

original_title: Room-Temperature Rhodium-Catalyzed Regioselective B(4)–H Hydroxylation of Pyridyl- o -carboranes Using Koser’s Reagent
title: Koser's reagent puts the hydroxyl right on carborane's B4
blurb: Rh-catalyzed, regioselective hydroxylation at the B(4) position of pyridyl-o-carboranes, run at room temperature with Koser's reagent instead of the usual harsh oxidants. Site-selective B-H functionalization on carboranes doesn't come easy, so the mild conditions are the point.

original_title: Photo-Fenton-Like (Trideutero)methylsulfonylation/Remote (Benzo)thiazolyl Migration Cascade of Unactivated Alkenes with DMSO(- d 6 )
title: DMSO-d6 feeds the CD3 group in a thiazolyl-migration cascade
blurb: A photo-Fenton-like cascade on unactivated alkenes that installs a trideuteromethylsulfonyl group and sends a (benzo)thiazolyl group on a remote migration in the same pot. DMSO-d6 as the deuterated methyl source is the neat part; the migration is where the real complexity gets built.

original_title: Diazoborane-Promoted Boron Insertion into Olefinic C–H Bonds
title: Boron goes straight into an olefin's C-H bond
blurb: A diazoborane reagent inserts boron directly into olefinic C-H bonds, bypassing the usual hydroboration across the double bond. A different disconnection for vinylboronates, worth watching if the scope turns out broad.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Phenanthrene-Based Triptycenes: Synthesis and Cascade Skeletal Transformations
title: Phenanthrene triptycenes rearrange themselves into new skeletons
blurb: Triptycenes built from phenanthrene units that don't just sit there as rigid scaffolds, they undergo cascade skeletal transformations into different ring systems. The synthesis is the setup; the rearrangement cascade is the actual news.

original_title: Cu-Catalyzed Tandem Cyclization of N-Benzyl 1,6-Diynes Via Vinyl Cations for Synthesis of Polycyclic N-Heterocycles
title: Vinyl cations chain diynes into polycyclic N-heterocycles
blurb: Cu-catalyzed tandem cyclization of N-benzyl 1,6-diynes running through vinyl cation intermediates, closing several rings in one pot to give polycyclic N-heterocycles. Vinyl cations put to actual synthetic use instead of staying a physical-organic curiosity.

original_title: Heterogeneous Palladium(0) Catalysts for C(sp 3 )–H Activation and Coupling to Form Hindered C–C Bonds
title: Heterogeneous Pd(0) takes on hindered C(sp3)-C bonds
blurb: Solid Pd(0) catalysts doing C(sp3)-H activation and coupling to stitch together sterically hindered C-C bonds, the kind that usually demand a homogeneous catalyst and a lot of patience. Heterogeneous means easier recovery and reuse, assuming the yields hold across substrates.

original_title: Osteogenic peptide-engineered Coptis chinensis-derived extracellular vesicles promote immuno-osteogenic repair of diabetic bone defects.
title: Coptis chinensis plant vesicles, dressed with a bone-building peptide, patch diabetic fractures
blurb: Extracellular vesicles from Coptis chinensis, engineered with an osteogenic peptide, repair diabetic bone defects by working the immune response and bone formation together. Plant-derived EVs as a delivery vehicle are still a niche approach, but diabetic bone defects are exactly the stubborn case that needs the immune assist.

original_title: Catalytic Decarboxylative Carboamination of 2-(Trifluoromethyl)acrylic Acid Using Oxime Esters via Photo/Thermal Relay
title: Oxime esters hand light off to heat for a decarboxylative carboamination
blurb: A catalytic decarboxylative carboamination of 2-(trifluoromethyl)acrylic acid using oxime esters as the nitrogen source, run through a photo/thermal relay where one step hands off to the other. Builds CF3-bearing amines while shedding CO2 as the leaving group.

original_title: Topology-Driven Molecular Recognition through Constitutional Isomerism
title: Same sugars, different links, and the binding changes completely
blurb: A multiscale computational comparison of cyclodextrins against their constitutional isomers, cyclodextrans, using force-field reparameterization, enhanced sampling, alchemical free energy runs, and QM energy decomposition to work out how just rearranging the same sugar linkages reprograms host-guest recognition. All computation, no synthesis, but a real crack at explaining why isomerism matters here at all.

original_title: Complete Computational Exploration of Eight-Carbon Hydrocarbon Chemical Space
title: Every eight-carbon hydrocarbon, mapped computationally
blurb: A full computational enumeration of the eight-carbon hydrocarbon chemical space, done in one sweep rather than piecemeal.

original_title: Copper Oxidation State Stabilization and Oxygen Vacancy Engineering in Perovskite Catalysts for Selective CO 2 -to-Ethylene Conversion
title: Tuning perovskite defects to push CO2 toward ethylene instead of CO
blurb: Perovskite catalysts engineered on two fronts, keeping copper's oxidation state stable and tuning oxygen vacancies, with the payoff being better selectivity for CO2 reduction toward ethylene over the usual CO.

original_title: Direct Electrochemical Synthesis of Tetrahydroisoquinolines through Shono-Type Oxidation
title: Electrochemistry builds tetrahydroisoquinolines, no divided cell required
blurb: Shono-type oxidation run electrochemically in a plain undivided cell with cheap, reusable electrodes, taking amides straight to multisubstituted tetrahydroisoquinolines across 34 examples, including electron-poor and heterocyclic substrates. The same setup also reaches the benzazepine scaffold.

original_title: Renal Function During and After (177)Lu-PSMA-617 Therapy in Patients with Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer.
title: Checking what Lu-177-PSMA-617 does to the kidneys, not just the tumor
blurb: Tracks renal function in men with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer before, during, and after a course of Lu-177-PSMA-617 therapy.

original_title: Microdroplet Separation Mass Spectrometry Resolves the Charge-Dependent Interfacial Catalytic Roles of Microdroplets
title: Splitting droplets apart to see which charge does the catalysis
blurb: A microdroplet-separation MS setup used to pull apart how charge state at the droplet interface drives the accelerated chemistry microdroplets are known for.

original_title: ROBUST TRANSFER LEARNING FOR BAYESIAN OPTIMIZATION OF CHEMICAL REACTIONS
title: Recycling old reaction-optimization runs to speed up new ones
blurb: Transfer learning grafted onto Bayesian optimization for reaction chemistry, aimed at the specific case of expanding a search space: you've already optimized a related but distinct set of substrates, ligands, catalysts, or objectives, and want the next campaign to start smart instead of from scratch.

original_title: Origin of Pt Nuclearity Dependence of CO Oxidation over Pt/TiO 2 Catalysts in the Subnanometer Regime
title: Why single Pt atoms and tiny clusters oxidize CO so differently
blurb: A mechanistic look at why CO oxidation on Pt/TiO2 shifts so sharply with cluster size once you're down in the subnanometer regime, single atoms versus a handful bound together.

original_title: Precise Fusion of Conjugated Organoboranes: A Tetraboron-Doped Molecular Carbon with Narrowband Fluorescence and Microscale Lasing
title: Four borons fused into a carbon nanographene, and it lases
blurb: A tetraboron-doped molecular carbon built by precisely fusing conjugated organoboranes, with narrowband fluorescence clean enough to drive microscale lasing.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Skeletal muscle radiodensity as an automated survival predictor in men with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer undergoing PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy.
title: Muscle density on CT predicts who survives PSMA therapy
blurb: Skeletal muscle radiodensity, read automatically off scans, used as a survival predictor in men with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer on PSMA-targeted radioligand therapy. An imaging biomarker pulled from data already being collected.
venue: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging

original_title: Beyond Thermodynamics-Determined Selectivity: Cation-Mediated Interfacial Microenvironment Promotes Desorption Kinetics To Enable Selective Hydroxylamine Electrosynthesis
title: Hydroxylamine electrosynthesis, tuned by kinetics not thermodynamics
blurb: Selectivity here isn't set by the usual thermodynamic sink. Cations parked at the interface reshape the local microenvironment and speed up desorption instead, and that kinetic lever is what steers the electrosynthesis toward hydroxylamine.

original_title: Novartis scraps mid-stage radiopharma asset as Pluvicto soars - BioSpace
title: Novartis kills a radiopharma program while Pluvicto keeps printing money
blurb: One mid-stage radioligand asset gets scrapped even as Pluvicto sales soar, which says more about that specific program's odds than about Novartis losing faith in radiopharma generally.

original_title: Vobramitamab duocarmazine, an anti-B7-H3 antibody-drug conjugate, in patients with advanced solid tumors: Final results of a phase 1 cohort expansion.
title: Final phase 1 numbers for the B7-H3 ADC
blurb: Vobramitamab duocarmazine, an anti-B7-H3 antibody-drug conjugate, reports its final phase 1 cohort expansion results in advanced solid tumors. Still just phase 1, so it's a first read on dosing and activity before anything randomized.

original_title: Beyond Thermal Limits: Manipulating Reactive Intermediate Coverages and Turnover Rates via Visible Photon-Mediated Catalysis on Rh-Doped Perovskite Oxides
title: Photons instead of heat to push turnover on Rh-doped perovskites
blurb: Visible-light photons, not extra heat, shift reactive-intermediate coverage and turnover rates on Rh-doped perovskite oxide catalysts, getting past the ceiling thermal activation alone runs into.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Alkali Hydride-Iron Oxide-Nanocomposites as Catalysts for Low-Temperature Ammonia Synthesis
title: An ammonia catalyst that skips the Haber-Bosch heat
blurb: Alkali hydride-iron oxide nanocomposites catalyze ammonia synthesis at low temperature, chasing the same nitrogen fixation Haber-Bosch does without the usual heat and pressure bill.

original_title: Chemical Synthesis of Asymmetric Nucleosomes Reveals Intramolecular Activation of UHRF1 E3 Ligase by H3K9me3.
title: Building asymmetric nucleosomes to catch UHRF1 turning itself on
blurb: Chemically synthesized nucleosomes marked on only one H3 tail show that H3K9me3 activates UHRF1's E3 ligase intramolecularly, not through some separate reader-writer handoff. Needed a synthetic construct to hold that asymmetry still long enough to see it.

original_title: London Dispersion in Confined Chiral Ion-Pair Asymmetric Catalysis: From Background Interaction to Design Principle
title: London dispersion, promoted from background noise to design rule
blurb: A conspectus arguing that in confined chiral ion-pair catalysis, dispersion forces usually treated as background noise deserve to be an actual design principle you engineer around. More a framework paper laying out the mechanistic case than a new reaction.

original_title: A loss-of-function polymorphism in the propeptide of lysyl oxidase exacerbates atherosclerosis.
title: A lysyl oxidase variant that makes atherosclerosis worse
blurb: A loss-of-function polymorphism sitting in the propeptide of lysyl oxidase exacerbates atherosclerosis, tying a specific LOX variant to worse plaque outcomes rather than just correlating with disease.

original_title: Guar gum-based multi-dynamic cross-linked network hydrogel with wound adaptability and microenvironment regulation for the treatment of burn infections.
title: A guar gum hydrogel that flexes to fit the burn wound
blurb: Multiple dynamic cross-links let this guar gum hydrogel conform to an irregular wound bed while also regulating the local microenvironment, built specifically for infected burns. A materials paper, so the biocompatibility and infection-clearance numbers are what would need to hold up beyond the bench.

original_title: Antigen-specific Antibody Multi-modal Foundation Model for Functional Antibody Design
title: A foundation model that actually conditions on the antigen
blurb: Most antibody language models generate sequences in isolation. AAMFM instead learns joint sequence-and-structure representations conditioned on the specific antigen, down to the epitope, which is what generic protein LMs skip. Whether that epitope-conditioning actually beats sequence-only baselines at designing binders is the number to look for.

original_title: Negative Fragmentation Approach to investigate the backbone hydrogen-bond energies for protein secondary structures
title: Quantifying exactly how much a backbone H-bond is worth in a helix vs a sheet
blurb: A BSSE-corrected fragmentation method puts real numbers on the backbone carbonyl-amide H-bonds holding together α-helices, π-helices, 3-10 helices, and both β-sheet geometries. Nice to see per-bond energies instead of the usual hand-wavy claim that H-bonding stabilizes secondary structure.

original_title: A radical approach to making C-glycoside molecules
title: Turning sugar into C-glycosides via a free-radical shortcut
blurb: Glycosyl sulfonyl hydrazides serve as easy-to-make radical precursors for a new platform that builds C-C bonds across the sugar scaffold with control over the resulting 3D arrangement. C-glycosides are usually miserable to make stereoselectively, so a targeted route is worth having if it holds up beyond the reported cases.

original_title: Catalysts for the Glycolysis of PET: High-Throughput Experimentation to Design a Thermoresponsive Approach
title: A PET-recycling catalyst that precipitates itself out when the job's done
blurb: High-throughput screening turned up thermoresponsive organocatalysts for PET glycolysis that stay dissolved and active at high temperature, then reversibly drop out of solution on cooling, making catalyst recovery close to free. Breaks PET down to BHET for repolymerization into virgin-quality plastic, which is the point of doing this chemically instead of just melting it down again.

original_title: Fluorescent Peptide Probe for Specific Detection and In Vivo Imaging of Fusobacterium Nucleatum
title: A fluorescent probe built to spot Fusobacterium nucleatum specifically
blurb: A peptide probe for specific detection and in vivo imaging of F. nucleatum, the gum bacterium that keeps showing up in colorectal tumors. Not much detail beyond that, but a specific in vivo imaging handle on this bug is useful given how often it's implicated without a good way to actually watch it.

original_title: Process modelling and thermodynamic analysis of cyanuric acid vinylation with acetylene
title: Actually process-modeling the industrial route to trivinyl cyanuric acid
blurb: An Aspen Plus steady-state model for vinylating cyanuric acid with acetylene, working through the stoichiometry, thermodynamics, and phase equilibrium across feed mixing and a continuous reactor at 90°C and 1.52 bar. Unglamorous, but apparently nobody had bothered modeling this reaction at the process level before.

original_title: Mechanically Interlocked Double-Walled Covalent Organic Framework with Cucurbit[8]Uril-Stabilized Viologen Radical Dimers
title: A double-walled COF held together by threaded viologen radical dimers
blurb: A mechanically interlocked, double-walled covalent organic framework where cucurbit[8]uril hosts stabilize viologen radical dimers built right into the structure. Clean mechanical interlocking is hard enough in small molecules, let alone a whole framework, so this is a structural flex more than anything.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Deciphering the mechanism of protein aggregation and effects of inhibitors using single-molecule mass photometry.
title: Watching protein aggregation happen one molecule at a time, then testing what stops it
blurb: Single-molecule mass photometry tracks the aggregation mechanism directly and tests how inhibitors interrupt it, molecule by molecule instead of averaged over a bulk sample. That's the whole appeal, actual oligomerization steps instead of an ensemble blur.
venue: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A

original_title: Molecular Engineering of Asymmetric Cyanines with Hybridized Local and Charge-Transfer Characteristics for NIR-II Imaging-Guided Type I/II Photodynamic Therapy
title: One cyanine dye doing both flavors of photodynamic therapy plus the imaging
blurb: Asymmetric cyanines engineered with mixed local-excitation and charge-transfer character pull double duty, NIR-II imaging alongside Type I and Type II photodynamic therapy from the same molecule. Getting one dye to run both PDT mechanisms and image in the NIR-II window is a fair amount of molecular engineering packed into one probe.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Hybrid Hydrogen-Bond Networks Steer Proton-Coupled Pathway for Acidic CO 2 -To-Ethanol Electrosynthesis
title: Steering CO2-to-ethanol electrosynthesis with a purpose-built hydrogen-bond network
blurb: A hybrid hydrogen-bond network at the electrode steers the proton-coupled electron transfer pathway toward ethanol specifically, and does it in acidic media, where CO2 reduction usually loses selectivity fast. Acidic-media electrolysis is the harder, more industrially relevant regime, so tuning selectivity there is the part worth noting.
venue: Journal of the American Chemical Society

original_title: Deep Eutectic Solvents: A Comprehensive Landscape of Two Decades of Research, Emerging Frontiers, and Translational Challenges (2003–2025)
title: Twenty years of deep eutectic solvents, tallied up
blurb: A corpus-wide review trying to synthesize two decades of DES literature into where the actual knowledge gaps are, rather than another topical summary telling you they're green and tunable. Useful as a map of the field; whether it changes what anyone funds next is another matter.

original_title: Impact of Microglial Activation on Synaptic Density in Alzheimer's Disease
title: Watching microglia while they watch your synapses disappear
blurb: An Alzheimer's imaging study pairing [11C]-UCB-J, a synaptic density tracer, with [18F]-DPA-714, which lights up activated microglia, to see whether the two track each other in the same patients. Trial's active but not recruiting anymore, so this is the design, not the answer yet.

original_title: Interfacial Cooperation-Driven Shifts of Volcano Relationships in Ammonia Decomposition
title: Moving the volcano's peak instead of climbing it
blurb: Ammonia decomposition catalysis usually gets plotted as a volcano against binding energy, with one best spot in the middle. This one argues cooperation at the interface between phases shifts where that peak sits, so a catalyst that looked mediocre alone can end up on top once paired.

original_title: mioXpektron: a modular Python toolkit for ToF-SIMS spectral recalibration, preprocessing and analysis
title: A python toolkit for the ToF-SIMS calibration errors nobody checks
blurb: mioXpektron bundles reference-ion recalibration, denoising, baseline correction, normalization, peak detection, integration, and alignment into one open-source pipeline. The pitch is that small channel-to-m/z errors quietly mangle peak assignment and cross-sample comparisons, so somebody had to write the plumbing properly.

original_title: Organocatalyzed Diastereo- and Site-Selective Monoacylation of Acyclic Diarylpropanoid Stereotriads by Amplification Processes
title: Acylating just one hydroxyl out of three, on purpose
blurb: An organocatalytic monoacylation that goes after acyclic diarylpropanoid stereotriads and picks both which site and which diastereomer reacts, using an amplification process to sharpen selectivity that would otherwise be a coin flip across near-identical OH groups.

original_title: Prospective comparison of [68Ga]Ga-FAPI-04 PET/MRI, [18F]FDG PET/CT, and contrast-enhanced MRI for predicting pathologic complete response after neoadjuvant chemotherapy in breast cancer
title: Three scans, one question: did the chemo actually work
blurb: A prospective single-center trial runs [68Ga]Ga-FAPI-04 PET/MRI, [18F]FDG PET/CT, and contrast-enhanced MRI on the same stage II-III breast cancer patients before and after neoadjuvant chemo, all aimed at predicting pathologic complete response ahead of surgery. FAPI-04 targets tumor stroma rather than glucose uptake, so it's a genuinely different bet than FDG, not just a fancier version of it.
venue: European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging

original_title: Enhancing Ion Yields in MALDI-2-MSI Using Dopant-Assisted Post Ionization for High-Resolution MS-Imaging of Lipids and Metabolites
title: Adding a dopant to MALDI-2's second laser pulse
blurb: MALDI-2 already reionizes the desorbed plume with a second UV pulse to rescue analytes that MALDI alone barely ionizes. This adds a dopant to that post-ionization step and reports higher signal for lipid and metabolite classes, aimed at sharper mass spectrometry imaging of tissue sections.

original_title: Data Integrity Frameworks
title: Data Integrity Frameworks
blurb: I teach chemistry at a regional university, and I wish to improve my instruction in record-keeping (related to my earlier post on electron lab manuals). I'm a biochemist, so I'm most familiar with the biochem side of things, but not all of my students are going in that direction, and I would like to make sure I'm covering all the bases. In pharmaceutical/healthcare-related research, ALCOA++ is the major data integrity framework. Is ALCOA++ used in areas that are unrelated to pharmaceutical/healthcare? If not, what other data integrity frameworks are used?

original_title: How Raman Spectroscopy and Chemistry Uncovered an $80 Million Art Forgery Scheme
title: How Raman Spectroscopy and Chemistry Uncovered an $80 Million Art Forgery Scheme
blurb: Most people think art authentication relies on historians, curators, and provenance records. But sometimes, chemistry provides the decisive answer. One of the most famous examples of this involves an alleged forgery operation linked to Chinese artist Pei-Shen Qian, whose paintings imitated styles of famous abstract expressionist masters. In this case, when experts examined a purported Jackson Pollock using Raman microscopy, their analysis detected Pigment Red 170. This synthetic pigment wasn't commercially available until decades after Pollock died in 1956. The chemistry simply didn't match th
